context: Tests whether the solver follows swift-language guidance for Swift 6.3 C interop, specialization, inlining, and module-selector syntax.

checklist.name: Rejects buffer pointer
checklist.description: States that UnsafeBufferPointer is a Swift struct and should not appear in an @c exported C-compatible signature.

checklist.name: C-compatible export
checklist.description: Shows an @c example using C-compatible pointer and scalar parameters, optionally with a custom C symbol name.

checklist.name: C type limits
checklist.description: Mentions that Swift-only types such as String, Array, closures, generic placeholders, or UnsafeBufferPointer do not belong in the exported signature.

checklist.name: Specialized spelling
checklist.description: Uses @specialized, not @specialize, for explicit generic specialization.

checklist.name: Inline guarantee
checklist.description: Explains that Swift 6.3 @inline(always) is a guarantee for direct-call inlining and can be diagnosed when impossible, rather than just a hint.

checklist.name: Module selector
checklist.description: Uses ModuleName::symbol syntax for module selectors.

checklist.name: No underscored fallback
checklist.description: Does not recommend @_cdecl or @_specialize as the primary modern spelling for the corrected examples.
